qa.overall_assessment: All 14 ACs pass. pytest 14/14 green in 0.39s. One out-of-scope defect found by Codex adversarial review: LOCK_FILE-as-directory leaks stderr despite dev report claiming suppression. This scenario is not covered by any AC and is outside the defined cycle scope. Dev report overclaim about 'Codex finding 3' completeness is flagged as a minor finding. Cycle scope: PASS. Out-of-scope defect: flagged below.

root_cause_verification.rationale: arch-6: new hook intercepts Agent dispatches and checks declared paths via git check-ignore. Verified via AC1a/1b (block), AC2/3/5 (no-op). arch-3: ENOSPC/EISDIR writes now guarded with if-not-printf and || patterns. Verified via AC7, AC7b.

build_verification.skip_reason: No compiled artifacts or deployed services. Changes are hook scripts and settings.json — no build step required.

regression_test_results.details: json.load succeeds; new hook appended to existing Agent matcher entry (not duplicate)

generated_tests.description: 14 ACs covering arch-6 hook and arch-3 ENOSPC patch

out_of_scope_observations.observation: LOCK_FILE-as-directory EISDIR leaks stderr. When ${COUNTER_FILE}.lock is pre-created as a directory, 'decision=$( { ... } 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null )' emits 'hooks/userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh: line 146: <lock_file>: Is a directory' to the hook's stderr. This is because Bash evaluates 9>"$LOCK_FILE" left-to-right before 2>/dev/null takes effect for the shell's own redirection setup error. Dev report claimed 'Codex finding 3 applied — EISDIR stderr suppressed on group redirect' but this is incorrect for the lock-file scenario. Confirmed by direct reproduction: rc=0 but stderr non-empty. The 14 defined ACs do not cover this scenario (they test COUNTER_FILE as directory, not LOCK_FILE as directory). The fix per Codex PROPOSED_FIX is: '} 2>/dev/null 9> "$LOCK_FILE"' (stderr redirect first). Not a blocker for cycle close since no AC covers this path. Recommend addressing in a follow-up cycle.

Dev report self_verification overclaims: 'Codex finding 3 (lock-file 2>/dev/null) applied' implies the EISDIR stderr leak on the group redirect is fully suppressed. QA directly reproduced the leak when LOCK_FILE is a directory. The fix is incomplete. This is a documentation accuracy issue, not a blocking AC violation.

all_findings.issue: LOCK_FILE-as-directory EISDIR leaks one stderr line. 'decision=$( { ... } 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null )' does not suppress Bash's fd-setup error when LOCK_FILE is a directory. Codex proposed fix: reorder to '} 2>/dev/null 9> "$LOCK_FILE"'. Confirmed reproducible by QA. Not covered by any defined AC.
all_findings.recommendation: Change line 129 from '} 9> "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null' to '} 2>/dev/null 9> "$LOCK_FILE"'. Add AC7c test that pre-creates LOCK_FILE as a directory and asserts rc=0, stdout='', stderr=''.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex session 019e5b8b found one MAJOR finding: 'arch-3 lock-file failure still leaks stderr; QA draft overclaims line 129 is safe.' Evidence: reproduced rc=0 but stderr='Is a directory' when LOCK_FILE is pre-created as a directory. PROPOSED_FIX: change group redirect from '} 9> LOCK_FILE 2>/dev/null' to '} 2>/dev/null 9> LOCK_FILE'. QA independently reproduced the finding.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex finding classified as minor out-of-scope finding. All 14 ACs remain PASS. Finding elevated to out_of_scope_observations[] and all_findings[] as minor/non-blocking. Verdict held as PASS per cycle scope (14 ACs). Finding routing: recommend dev follow-up to fix redirect order and add AC7c.

qa.verdict: All 14 acceptance criteria verified and pass. pytest 14/14 green. One minor out-of-scope defect found by Codex adversarial review (LOCK_FILE-as-directory EISDIR leaks stderr through group redirect; dev report overclaims suppression). This defect does not breach any defined AC and is not blocking. Recommend a follow-up cycle to fix redirect order and add AC7c coverage. Cycle scope: PASS.
